tasks.title: WAVE11 Performance: Make effective DB pool and connection-lifecycle posture explicit
tasks.prompt: Use `lib/design/design-plan.json` and `../socialpredict/README/PRODUCTION-NOTES/BACKEND/11-runtime-performance-tuning.md` as the binding context for WAVE11 runtime performance tuning. Treat upstream `main` at commit `051aac6b2fefa5634b8c98cc38caf52acf0043a9` as the reviewed baseline. Start by making the runtime-owned `sql.DB` pool and lifetime settings explicit and observable: normalize effective values, log them safely at startup, and document the current env knobs per deployment shape without changing query architecture. Preserve current ownership in `backend/internal/app/runtime/db.go` and do not introduce a new performance subsystem.
acceptance_criteria: The touched runtime path makes effective pool and connection-lifecycle values explicit without leaking secrets or changing unrelated DB behavior.
Focused tests cover normalization or default-selection behavior for the touched pool settings.
Operator-facing docs or touched runtime notes reflect the current performance knobs honestly instead of promising a broader optimization platform.

focus: runtime ownership of DB pool and lifecycle settings
safe observability of effective performance knobs

out_of_scope: query rewrites or index changes before evidence exists
notes: Expose effective DB-pool posture safely; never log secrets or full DSNs.
Keep the first slice focused on the owned runtime knobs that already exist.
tasks.last_summary:
task_uid: `019de369-df09-7241-835a-e8c54bbbcb78`  
task_id: `WAVE11-PERF-001`  
status: `complete`

Changed:
- `backend/internal/app/runtime/db.go`
- `backend/internal/app/runtime/db_test.go`
- `backend/logger/simplelogging.go`
- `README/PRODUCTION-NOTES/BACKEND/11-runtime-performance-tuning.md`

Summary:
- Added explicit effective DB pool posture normalization for max open, max idle, connection lifetime, and idle time.
- Startup now logs `db_pool.configured` with safe effective values only, without DSNs, host/user/db names, passwords, or secrets.
- Added focused runtime tests for defaults, invalid env fallback, negative normalization, and idle cap behavior.
- Updated the performance tuning note with current env knobs, defaults, safe observability, and deployment-shape guidance.

Checks run:
- `go test ./internal/app/runtime`: PASS
- reliability suite for `./internal/app/runtime`: PASS
- architecture boundary checks: PASS
- dispatcher-reported API contract sync, `gofmt`, and `go vet`: PASS

Follow-ups:

- Report summary is updated at `/workspace/socialpredict/.codex-reports/tasks/019de369-df09-7241-835a-e8c54bbbcb78/summary.json`.

tasks.title: WAVE11 Performance: Add the smallest repeatable measurement seam for pool saturation and latency
tasks.prompt: Before tuning values, add a small repeatable measurement seam close to the owning runtime or server packages, such as targeted benchmarks, opt-in real-Postgres checks, or deterministic instrumentation around pool waits and readiness, so pool changes can be compared against evidence. Do not create a generalized load-testing platform, always-on profiling program, or `pprof` rollout.
acceptance_criteria: The repo contains a repeatable measurement seam tied to the current runtime or server packages for the touched pool-related behavior.
That seam can differentiate at least one saturation or latency dimension affected by pool tuning.
The measurement stays package-local or narrowly scoped instead of becoming a broad performance-testing framework.

focus: repeatable evidence for pool-related runtime behavior
narrow measurement ownership in runtime or server packages

out_of_scope: broad load or stress testing platforms
notes: Prefer opt-in real-Postgres checks where SQLite cannot prove the behavior.
The goal is comparative evidence for tuning, not a full benchmark suite.
tasks.last_summary:

tasks.title: WAVE11 Performance: Set initial deployment defaults for DB pool knobs from measured behavior
tasks.prompt: Use evidence from the measurement seam to choose initial `DB_MAX_*` and connection-lifetime defaults for the current deployment shapes, update the current env or compose templates, and explain the tradeoff in operator-facing docs. Keep the change reversible, tie it directly to the measured evidence, and do not speculate about caching, background workers, or query rewrites.
acceptance_criteria: The touched env or deployment templates encode explicit initial pool defaults or documented overrides for the current backend runtime.
The chosen values are tied to the measured behavior from the preceding slice instead of guesswork.
Touched docs make it clear when those values should be revisited and what signal would justify doing so.

focus: alignment between runtime knobs and deployment templates
reversible performance tuning without speculative scope growth

out_of_scope: query or index changes without measured justification
deployment-platform redesign beyond touched runtime defaults
notes: Keep deployment defaults explicit where the repo already owns them.
Do not let a lack of perfect data justify speculative performance settings.
tasks.last_summary:

what changed:
- Encoded explicit initial DB pool/lifetime defaults: `DB_MAX_OPEN_CONNS=25`, `DB_MAX_IDLE_CONNS=5`, `DB_CONN_MAX_LIFETIME=30m`, `DB_CONN_MAX_IDLE_TIME=5m`.
- Added production compose defaults for both `backend-startup-writer` and `backend`, with env overrides.
- Documented why the defaults stay aligned with current runtime behavior: WAVE11-PERF-002 showed `max_open=1` saturates while `max_open=2` clears the controlled seam, but did not justify speculative changes beyond existing defaults.
- Documented revisit signals using `/ops/status.dbPool` wait counters, request/readiness symptoms, DB connection capacity, and lifecycle closure counters.

tasks.title: WAVE11 Performance: Stop and review the first measured hotspot and next evidence-backed seam
tasks.prompt: Finish WAVE11 with a performance-specific stop-and-review point. Summarize what the pool-tuning work changed, record whether the first bottleneck moved or disappeared, and only name a concrete query or index follow-up if the measurements justify it. Keep caching, background jobs, compression middleware, and broader performance-platform work deferred.
acceptance_criteria: The active performance note accurately reflects the measured tuning outcome for the touched runtime seam.
If a bottleneck remains, the next follow-up is stated as a concrete migration-owned query or index seam rather than a speculative optimization backlog.
Caching, worker, and platform-heavy performance ideas remain explicitly deferred at the end of the wave.

focus: accuracy of the post-wave performance inventory
evidence-backed identification of the next hotspot if one remains
continued deferral of speculative optimization programs

notes: This is the WAVE11 stop-and-review point before any query or index work is queued.
Only queue a follow-up hotspot if the measurement seam actually justifies it.
tasks.last_summary:
